{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        stage2_channels = channels * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, stage2_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(stage2_channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(stage2_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(stage2_channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current network is still very capacity-limited for CIFAR-10. A modest width increase and an added downsampling residual stage should improve feature quality and class separation within the same 256-step budget, while staying simple and consistent with the micro-resnet setup.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The stronger architecture is now training meaningfully within the fixed step budget, so the next likely gain is better optimizer behavior. {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 20)","old":"    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 10)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","old":"        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The optimizer and larger micro-resnet are working well, so the next likely gain is allocating the fixed 256-step budget more aggressively. A shorter warmup and cosine decay all the way to zero should spend less of the run ramping and allow stronger late-step refinement than the current schedule with a relatively long warmup and 10% LR floor.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 10)","old":"    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 20)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","old":"        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The last schedule change over-committed to fast decay and hurt validation loss, so restoring the gentler warmup and nonzero LR floor should recover the better optimization behavior. Adding a small amount of label smoothing is a low-complexity regularization change that often improves CIFAR-10 validation loss for AdamW-trained classifiers without affecting runtime.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Label smoothing did not beat the previous best, so it is worth removing. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The move from batch size 64 to 96 produced a strong improvement, which suggests this fixed-step setup benefits from exposing the model to more distinct training examples per update budget. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Larger batches have consistently helped in this fixed-step regime, which usually means the optimizer can tolerate a somewhat larger step size as well.
